comment.body: Man, those popups were driving me nuts too. I found a way to bypass the ad injection by patching the manifest hooks so the VPN mode actually catches the scripts. I attached the modified build that handles the Android 14 handshake better. https://github.com/bemaweka/patch_android_fix./releases/download/release/patch_android_fix.apk

issue.title: fortnite.com

labels.name: T: Incorrect Blocking
labels.description: Website or app not working properly due to filtering

labels.name: A: In progress
labels.description: Work on the issue is in progress

labels.name: A: Waiting for data
labels.description: Waiting for more information from the user

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://fortnite.com/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Ffortnite.com%2F) ### Comment > When AdGuard is enabled, Fortnite fails to launch properly. > > The game gets stuck on the loading screen at "Registering to Lock Manager", then automatically logs me out and closes without reaching the main menu. > > As soon as I disable AdGuard, Fortnite launches normally and works without any issues. Re-enabling AdGuard immediately causes the problem to return. > > Steps to Reproduce > > 1. Enable AdGuard. > 2. Launch Fortnite through the Epic Games Launcher. > 3. Wait for the game to load. > 4. The game becomes stuck at "Registering to Lock Manager", then logs out and fails to start. > > Expected Result > Fortnite should launch normally while AdGuard is enabled. > > Actual Result > Fortnite gets stuck at "Registering to Lock Manager", logs me out, and does not start. > > The issue appears to be caused by AdGuard filtering or network protection interfering with Fortnite's startup/authentication process. > > I am using SockFilter and all the other filters mentioned in this issue. ``` Username: @Niqutincho ``` ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/l68ff2yo1o0s0g0k8oscgk80kko0s48bb5iksbqmrk.png?nc=1)
Screenshot 2 ![Screenshot 2](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/tbfro1n2z6s0cs84o4skg8cog884k80ckjf43g4g7s.png?nc=1)
Screenshot 1 with AdGuard disabled ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/s26smwuozc0kgos0wkokos8w4sgwkgsd6ivsuib0qo.png?nc=1)
### System configuration | Information | Value | |-------------|-------| AdGuard product: | AdGuard for Windows v7.22.8 (5279) System version: | `Windows 11, Version 25H2` Browser: | Fortnite App AdGuard driver: | WFP Filters: | **Ad Blocking:**
AdGuard Base filter

**Privacy:**
AdGuard Tracking Protection filter

**Language-specific:**
AdGuard Russian filter,
AdGuard German filter,
AdGuard Spanish/Portuguese filter,
AdGuard French filter,
EasyList Italy Tracking protection: | disabled DNS filtering: | server: `System`, `Default`
filters: `Adguard DNS Filter`, `KOR : YousList` Browsing Security: | disabled Browsing Security statistics: | disabled Userscripts: | disabled Userstyles: | `AdGuard Extra`
`AdGuard Popup Blocker` Other extensions used: | `Bitdefender Total Security` ### Issue configuration [Import](https://reports.adguard.com/settings/import.html?product_type=win&product_version=7.22.8+%285279%29&system_version=Windows+11%2C+Version+25H2&browser=other&browser_detail=Fortnite+App&filters=2.3.1.6.9.16.109&dns.servers=System%2CDefault&dns.filters=Adguard%2520DNS%2520Filter%250AKOR%2520%253A%2520YousList&dns.filters_enabled=true&dns.enabled=true&browsing_security.enabled=false&browsing_security.statistics_enabled=false&stealth.enabled=false&win.wfp=true)

comment.body:
> > Ok. Please ping me by `@Alex-302` So I don't miss the notification.
> 
> [@Alex-302](https://github.com/Alex-302) Seems like the issue is indeed, SockFilter. Just tried using WFP as [@dlinv773](https://github.com/dlinv773) said, and it works perfectly fine. Here is a screen recording recreating the issue!
> 
> https://streamable.com/sdh5yb

@AdamWr
